title: DevLevelUp
shortDescription: Gamified daily-lesson platform for learning web development, built solo end-to-end and sold to a client.
fullDescription: DevLevelUp is a full-stack learning platform that breaks web development into daily, bite-sized lessons instead of long, easy-to-abandon courses. The system is split into three parts I built and connected myself: an Express/PostgreSQL REST API, a React + Vite dashboard app where students actually learn, and a separate Astro marketing/landing site. Learners move through a Course → Module → Lesson hierarchy, take quizzes, and track progress per lesson and module, all wired to Clerk for authentication with webhook-based user syncing to the database. On top of the core LMS, I layered a gamification system — XP logs and unlockable achievements — to keep learners coming back daily, plus a personal study layer of drag-and-drop notes and folders so students could organize their own takeaways. An admin side of the app gives course/lesson/user management and analytics dashboards. It started as my own personal project; I later sold the finished platform to a client.
problem: Most self-taught developers rely on long, generic course platforms where it's easy to enroll and never finish. There's no structure that breaks learning into small daily habits, tracks fine-grained progress per lesson, or rewards showing up consistently — so motivation drops off long before the course is complete.
solution: DevLevelUp restructures learning around small daily lessons grouped into modules and courses, with quizzes and progress tracking at every level. XP and achievements turn consistent daily learning into a game, while personal notes and folders let students build their own reference material as they go — all backed by a proper multi-role system (student and admin) instead of a single flat course viewer.
impact: Designed and built the entire stack solo: an Express/PostgreSQL REST API, a React + Vite student/admin dashboard, and a separate Astro marketing site, unified under Clerk authentication with webhook-based user sync.
Modeled a full Course → Module → Lesson hierarchy with per-lesson and per-module progress tracking, enrollments, and quizzes with graded answers.
Built a gamification layer on top of the core LMS — XP logs and unlockable achievements — to encourage daily learning habits.
Added a personal study layer of drag-and-drop notes and folders (via dnd-kit) so students could organize their own lesson takeaways.
Shipped an admin side with course/lesson/user management and analytics dashboards.
Delivered end-to-end in ~3 months as a solo developer, then sold the finished platform to a client.
